meta.title: On-Device Frontier LLM Inference by 2030
meta.question: Will a model running fully on-device on a consumer smartphone (commercially available at retail price <=1500 USD, no cloud offload) achieve performance within 15% of the then-best commercially available cloud frontier API model on >=3 contemporaneous independent technical evaluations by December 31, 2030?
meta.resolution: Resolved YES if: (a) a consumer smartphone commercially available at <=1500 USD runs an LLM fully on-device without cloud offload; (b) >=3 independent technical evaluations confirm performance within 15% of the contemporaneous cloud frontier model using benchmark suites current at time of evaluation (not pegged to 2026 benchmarks). Resolved NO if no such product exists by 2030-12-31.

tagConfig._docs: CAPABILITY/HARDWARE favor H1/H2. PLATEAU/REGULATORY favor H3/H4.

H1.label: Fast path: consumer device with on-device frontier ships by 2028
H1.description: Rapid hardware scaling + quantization/distillation closes the gap faster than expected. A consumer device running a model competitive with contemporaneous cloud frontier ships commercially by end of 2028. Requires NPU leap or parameter-efficiency breakthrough not on current roadmaps.

H2.label: Consensus path: on-device frontier ships commercially 2029-2030
H2.description: Steady hardware + model efficiency gains converge at or before 2030. Most likely path: 2028-generation NPU enables competitive on-device inference by 2029-2030. Aligned with @ponder.ooo prediction (cold_004).

H3.label: Gap persists past 2030: hardware viable 2031-2033
H3.description: Hardware scales but frontier cloud models scale faster, maintaining the gap. Consumer phones eventually reach the required threshold, but not within 2030. On-device of older-gen models is feasible; within-15%-of-contemporaneous-frontier remains elusive until early 2030s.

H4.label: Decade-long barrier: structural limits prevent parity before 2034
H4.description: Structural barriers -- battery-constrained thermal envelope, bandwidth requirements, or fundamental quality/size tradeoffs -- prevent consumer smartphones from reaching contemporaneous frontier parity before 2034. Cloud offload remains architecturally necessary for frontier-class performance.

posteriorHistory.note: Initial priors. YES-by-2030 = 45% (H1+H2). NOT-by-2030 = 55% (H3+H4). H1 fast path lower weight; H2 consensus path. H3/H4 informed by hardware roadmap analysis. cold_004 (@ponder.ooo) seeds H2 as a PREDICTION.
Clear secular narrowing on desktop hardware supports H2 (consumer-frontier 2029-2030) and modestly raises H1 odds; mobile bandwidth gap keeps H3 alive.

redTeam.challenge: No significant shifts proposed; no challenge needed.

posteriorHistory.note: On-device 8B at 73 MMLU plus ~25-30x bandwidth deficit = consensus 2029-30 commercialization track (H2).

redTeam.challenge: No significant shifts proposed; no challenge needed.

posteriorHistory.note: Hardware scaling toward 30-50 TOPS fits 2029-2030 commercial-frontier path (H2); not yet 2028.

redTeam.challenge: No significant shifts proposed; no challenge needed.

tier1_critical.desc: Consumer smartphone (<=1500 USD) commercially available before 2029-01-01 demonstrated running on-device LLM within 15% of contemporaneous cloud frontier on >=3 independent evaluations. Observable via product launch + benchmark reports.
Same criteria as t1_frontier_parity_before_2029 but product commercially available 2029-01-01 through 2030-12-31.

tier2_strong.desc: Consumer smartphone flagship ships with >=16GB unified memory accessible to on-device inference AND >=100 TOPS NPU (Apple, Qualcomm, or MediaTek). Observable via published product specs.
Peer-reviewed or major-lab publication demonstrates quantization/distillation achieving <10% quality degradation vs full-precision frontier at >=8x compression, independently verified.
Independent benchmark (Epoch AI or peer) shows best on-device consumer-GPU model within 3 months of contemporaneous cloud frontier on >=3 evals. Leading indicator for smartphone path via downscale.

tier3_suggestive.desc: Open-weight model scoring >=80% of contemporaneous frontier runs on consumer smartphone without cloud offload in reproducible independent test.
Apple, Qualcomm, or MediaTek announces a public roadmap targeting >=4x on-device inference throughput improvement within 3 years.
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: H2 +3pp, H3 -2pp, H4 -2pp. H1 unchanged (3yr horizon implies not before 2029).

tier3_suggestive.desc: >=3 independent hardware analysts (IDC, Gartner, Forrester, SemiAnalysis, or equivalent credible technical analysts) publish reports placing on-device frontier-class inference feasibility in the 2031-2033 window specifically, based on current NPU and memory roadmaps.
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: H3 +10pp, H4 -10pp. H1 and H2 unaffected (this addresses post-2030 timing only).

tier3_suggestive.desc: Frontier-tier model (within 20% of contemporaneous cloud frontier on >=2 evals) demonstrated on consumer smartphone with <=5B active parameters via sparse-MoE architecture, reproducible.
Consumer smartphone (<=1500 USD) on-device model reaches >=50% of contemporaneous cloud frontier on >=2 independent evals. Bridges t2 hardware spec and t3_open_weight_phone_80pct.

anti_indicators.desc: Anthropic, OpenAI, or Google publish technical analysis concluding frontier-class capability requires compute budgets incompatible with consumer smartphone thermal/power constraints through 2028.
Three consecutive Apple/Qualcomm flagship generations ship without >=12GB unified memory accessible to on-device inference.
Published research confirms on-device models (any hardware, no cloud offload) have closed to within 20% of cloud frontier on contemporaneous evaluations.
Three consecutive flagship smartphone generations show <10pp improvement in best on-device % of contemporaneous frontier.

actorModel.description: Hardware OEMs and AI labs drive the outcome. Model efficiency researchers are the key wildcard.

actor1.name: Key Decision Maker
actor1.role: Primary actor whose decisions drive the outcome

apple.name: Apple Inc.
apple.role: Consumer device OEM; controls Neural Engine NPU roadmap
biases: On-device privacy narrative is a product differentiator

qualcomm.name: Qualcomm
qualcomm.role: Android flagship SoC vendor; Snapdragon AI roadmap
qualcomm.decisionStyle: Competitive roadmap tied to OEM partnerships

methodology: ACTIONS OVER RHETORIC — only verified events move posteriors
TAG EVERYTHING — separate rhetoric from action in evidence log
DON'T FRONT-RUN — set tripwires and let news confirm
SOCIALIZATION DETECTION — recognize narrative prep patterns

evidenceLog.text: Social post prediction: 'local opus on a phone by 2030' -- claim that Opus-class LLM will run fully on-device on a consumer smartphone by end of 2030. Passes all 3 PREDICTION filters (specific, testable, time-bounded).
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. Prediction -- does not move posteriors. Logged for source calibration. Resolves 2030-12-31.

prediction.claim: Opus-class LLM runs locally on a consumer phone by 2030

evidenceLog.note: Retroactive from cold_004. Source trust 0.50 (unknown prior).

evidenceLog.text: DEADLINE MIGRATION: resolution_deadline stamped on ['H1', 'H2', 'H3', 'H4'] based on topic startDate 2026-04-21.
LR MIGRATION RESET: posteriors reset to design priors; evidence log preserved.
REPLAY BEGIN: 0 previously-fired indicators will be re-applied chronologically under current LR system. Posteriors reset to design priors; indicator state cleared. Evidence log preserved for audit.
REPLAY COMPLETE: 0 indicators applied, 0 skipped. Final posteriors: {'H1': 0.08, 'H2': 0.37, 'H3': 0.38, 'H4': 0.17}. Indicator firedDates restored to originals for audit.

Llama 4 Scout 109B MoE (17B active) with 10M token context window runs on single 24GB consumer GPU with native multimodal support; RTX 5090 runs models matching 6-12 months-ago frontier; EXO framework enables pooling VRAM across heterogeneous devices.

GOVERNANCE WARNING on bayesian_update: confidence_inflation, quorum_failure. Reason: 6-12 month frontier-to-consumer gap trend supports H2 (2029-2030 on-device frontier); slight update upward on H1 from EXO/quantization.

2026 state-of-union: single RTX 5090 (~$2.5k MSRP, current street $2.9-5k) can locally run models matching frontier quality from 6-12 months prior. Gemma 4 MoE (26B total / 4B active) runs at 85 tok/s on AMD Ryzen AI MAX+ 128GB. Mobile NPUs now approach V100-era TOPS. MoE dominates 7/10 top local models. Epoch AI estimates consumer-GPU-to-frontier gap narrowing to <1 year.
evidenceLog.source: Chandra On-Device LLMs / Epoch AI / Tom's Hardware

evidenceLog.text: EPISTEMIC CLAMP: point-estimate posteriors clamped to [0.005, 0.98].

GOVERNANCE WARNING on bayesian_update: confidence_inflation, quorum_failure. Reason: Consumer-to-frontier gap tightening to <1 year plus MoE efficiency gains lift H1 fast-path odds while H2 remains modal.

Epoch AI data: single RTX 5090 (<$2500) runs open models matching frontier performance from 6-12 months prior. DeepSeek 4/24 previews model 'closing gap' with frontier. Smartphone (<=$1500, no cloud) on-device frontier parity NOT yet demonstrated.
EPISTEMIC CLAMP: point-estimate posteriors clamped to [0.005, 0.98].
GOVERNANCE WARNING on bayesian_update: quorum_failure. Reason: GPU-class local frontier parity real but smartphone target remains harder — consistent with 2029-2030 consensus path.

Alibaba released Qwen 3.6-35B-A3B (April 14, 2026) - sparse MoE with 35B total / 3B active params, designed for compute-efficient frontier-tier deployment; runs on prosumer hardware. Apple, Qualcomm advancing on-device NPU stacks.
EPISTEMIC CLAMP: point-estimate posteriors clamped to [0.005, 0.98].
REPLAY BEGIN: 0 previously-fired indicators will be re-applied chronologically under current LR system. Posteriors reset to design priors; indicator state cleared. Evidence log preserved for audit.
REPLAY COMPLETE: 0 indicators applied, 0 skipped. Final posteriors: {'H1': 0.08, 'H2': 0.37, 'H3': 0.38, 'H4': 0.17}. Indicator firedDates restored to originals for audit.

Epoch AI April 2026: a single RTX 5090 (~$2.5k) can locally run models matching frontier LLM performance from 6-12 months ago. MoE architectures (Llama 4, DeepSeek V3.2, Qwen 3.5, Gemma 4, Mistral Small 4) dominate frontier with sparse routing. Quantization (Q4_K_M+) cuts VRAM 50-75% with low quality loss. But mobile bandwidth (50-90 GB/s) vs DC (2-3 TB/s) leaves a 30-50x decode-bound gap.
evidenceLog.source: Epoch AI, on-device LLM survey 2026, llama.cpp/ollama community

evidenceLog.text: GOVERNANCE WARNING on bayesian_update: confidence_inflation, quorum_failure. Reason: Clear secular narrowing on desktop hardware supports H2 (consumer-frontier 2029-2030) and modestly raises H1 odds; mobile bandwidth gap keeps H3 alive.

April 2026 on-device LLM landscape: mobile devices have 50-90 GB/s memory bandwidth vs 2-3 TB/s data-center GPUs; Llama 3.3 8B Q4_K_M scores 73 on MMLU on consumer hardware (GPT-4-class only 2 yrs ago); each Snapdragon generation yields ~50% prefill / >100% decode improvement. NPU-prefill + CPU/GPU-decode hybrid emerging as standard.
evidenceLog.source: v-chandra State of On-Device LLMs / arXiv 2410.03613

evidenceLog.text: EPISTEMIC CLAMP: point-estimate posteriors clamped to [0.005, 0.98].
GOVERNANCE WARNING on bayesian_update: confidence_inflation, quorum_failure. Reason: On-device 8B at 73 MMLU plus ~25-30x bandwidth deficit = consensus 2029-30 commercialization track (H2).

April 2026 SemiEngineering / aimagicx: Apple M4 + A18 Neural Engines deliver up to 38 TOPS; Snapdragon X Elite / 8 Gen 4 Hexagon NPU up to 45 TOPS; on-device 'goldilocks' is 3-30B parameters. Industry consensus: cloud still required for frontier reasoning, broad world knowledge, long multi-turn.
EPISTEMIC CLAMP: point-estimate posteriors clamped to [0.005, 0.98].

on_device_frontier_gap.label: On-device best model quality vs cloud frontier (%)
on_device_frontier_gap.source: Estimated from Phi-4/Gemma-3 vs Opus/GPT-4o benchmarks

dependencies._docs: No upstream dependencies. Potentially upstream for future AI-ubiquity/always-on-agent topics.

predictionScoring._docs: Calibration infrastructure. Snapshots are auto-created by engine on each posterior update. Record outcome when topic resolves, then compute_calibration_report() for Brier analysis.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF consensus path (on-device frontier 2029-2030), THEN >=1 major chip vendor (Qualcomm, Apple, or similar) announces consumer device with on-device frontier LLM capability by Q4 2029
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Observable: Public announcement from chip vendor of shipping consumer device with integrated frontier-class LLM inference (measured by capability benchmark or lab claims)

AMBER.reasoning: Discrete vendor announcement is a good threshold event. 0.71 fine.

BLUE.reasoning: Qualcomm/Apple announcement is standard vendor roadmap event. BLUE-compatible as predictable institutional milestone.

RED.reasoning: Vendor ANNOUNCEMENTS are easy — 'frontier-class' is vaporware until benchmarked. RED: vendors will announce; actual on-device frontier parity will lag. Also: 'or lab claims' resolution weakens this. Announcement bar is too low.
RED.modified_suggestion: Require independent benchmark confirmation, not lab self-report.

VIOLET.reasoning: Named chip vendors (Qualcomm, Apple) — this is agency-grounded. Cook/Amon roadmaps drive outcomes.

GRAY.reasoning: Binary announcement observable; clear falsifier by 2029Q4.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF consensus path holds, THEN on-device frontier inference power consumption remains <5W at inference time by commercial shipping (2029-2030)
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Observable: Technical specifications from vendor documentation show on-device frontier model inference power draw <5W for single-token generation

AMBER.reasoning: <5W is very specific and may be gamed by vendor marketing ('average', 'token-amortized', 'after first token' etc). Specify measurement protocol.
AMBER.modified_suggestion: Specify sustained power draw over a 1000-token generation under MLPerf-style measurement.

BLUE.reasoning: Power-consumption spec is measurable. Reasonable from BLUE lens.

RED.reasoning: <5W for frontier inference is aggressive — RED view: thermal/power constraints are harder than believed. Probability 0.65 too high; 0.45 more realistic.

GRAY.reasoning: Power-draw spec is technical and checkable against datasheets.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF gap persists (hardware viable 2031-2033), THEN published research through 2029 shows fundamental hardware challenges (memory bandwidth, power density) require 2+ more years of innovation
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Observable: Academic or industry white papers (Qualcomm, ARM, Apple, NVIDIA) published through 2029 document structural hardware barriers preventing on-device frontier inference before 2031

AMBER.reasoning: White-paper publication tracking — framework within-continuation observable.

BLUE.reasoning: White-paper documentation is institutional pattern. Reasonable.

RED.reasoning: RED-aligned: hardware gaps persist. Good prediction.

GRAY.reasoning: 'Published research documents structural barriers' is cherry-pickable; any survey can find such claims.
GRAY.modified_suggestion: Narrow to quantitative benchmark — e.g., no shipping SoC meets FP8 memory bandwidth threshold X GB/s by 2029-12-31.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF H2 (consensus path: on-device frontier ships 2029-2030), THEN market adoption lags device availability by 12-18 months due to app-developer supply constraints; developers lack tools and economic incentives to optimize for on-device frontier models. App-store ecosystem shows <15% of top-1000 apps shipping on-device inference by 2030-12-31, despite hardware availability.
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Observable: App-store analysis (iOS App Store, Google Play) as of 2030-12-31: sample 1,000 top apps by download count. Measure fraction with on-device LLM inference capability disclosed in feature lists or privacy labels. <15% indicates adoption lag. Cross-check against device-API adoption curves (iOS CoreML on-device model availability).

AMBER.reasoning: Adoption-lag via supply-side constraints is a solid regime-change observable.

BLUE.reasoning: Developer/app adoption lag is institutional-inertia pattern BLUE recognizes. Reasonable.

GRAY.reasoning: App-store sampling is replicable; 15% threshold is meaningful.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF H3 (gap persists 2031-2033), THEN cloud-centric moat deepens: frontier model inference remains cloud-hosted monopoly; cost structure (NVIDIA opex, power, real-estate) stabilizes cloud inference marginal cost at 40-60% below on-device inference despite on-device hardware parity, driven by economies of scale lock-in and amortized R&D. Geopolitical competition drives funding toward cloud-centralization (China domestic cloud, US cloud primacy).
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Observable: Compare marginal inference cost (per-token) cloud vs on-device 2030-2033. Cloud cost 40-60% below on-device due to amortized opex, batch processing economies, power pricing, and real-estate consolidation. Industry reports (Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs, Gartner AI Infrastructure) document cost structure advantage persisting despite hardware parity.

BLUE.reasoning: Cloud cost moat persistence is economic/institutional equilibrium. Reasonable.

RED.reasoning: Cloud moat deepening is sharp RED institutional lock-in thesis.

GRAY.reasoning: Cost-structure divergence is observable via industry reports; long horizon appropriate for deep-moat claim.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF decade-long barrier holds, THEN >=2 major model labs publicly abandon on-device frontier and re-commit to cloud-centric architectures by 2031-06-30.
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Public statements from >=2 major model labs explicitly abandoning on-device frontier targets.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF consensus path holds and on-device frontier inference ships 2029-2030 (H2), THEN hardware milestones are met via Apple A19/M5-generation architectures integrating 200B or more parameter models at acceptable latency (less than 2s first-token), with commercial shipping in Apple/Samsung flagship devices by Q3 2029.
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Apple or Samsung announces on-device frontier model with more than 150B parameters in chipset technical specs; benchmarks show less than 2s first-token latency on standard inputs; devices ship commercially in more than 50 million units within 6 months.

criticVerdicts.reasoning: 200B+ parameter models at <2s first-token by Q3 2029 is an acceleration scenario, not drift-forward. 15-20x parameter jump in ~3 Apple generations is aggressive.
criticVerdicts.modified_suggestion: Reduce to 0.50. Lower parameter threshold to 70B+ or reframe as GPT-4-class benchmark performance.

criticVerdicts.reasoning: Resolution deadline Q3 2029 is before upper bound of H2 conditioning window (2030). Supply chain brittleness: advanced packaging requires TSMC N2 yields; geopolitical disruption could sever this. 50M unit threshold extremely aggressive. 0.66 too high.
criticVerdicts.modified_suggestion: Lower to 0.44. Extend deadline to 2030-06-30. Add supply chain/export control failure mode as explicit disqualifier.

criticVerdicts.reasoning: Apple has strong incentive to gate frontier inference to premium devices (margin protection), not commodity flagships. Samsung's incentive structure differs. Prediction conflates two actors with different incentive structures.
criticVerdicts.modified_suggestion: Lower to 0.55 or add Apple Pro-line-only resolution path. Clarify Apple's incentive is to differentiate Pro tiers.

criticVerdicts.reasoning: 200B+ parameter on-device requirement at <2s first-token by Q3 2029 assumes memory bandwidth scaling not guaranteed by current DRAM/HBM roadmaps. Semiconductor supply chain path-dependence (TSMC 2nm yield curves, HBM3E constraints) is the binding constraint.
criticVerdicts.modified_suggestion: Reduce to 0.48. Modify to 70B+ at <2s latency — consistent with projected memory bandwidth by 2029. Retain 50M unit shipment criterion.

criticVerdicts.reasoning: Specific, hardware-grounded, and testable. Apple A19/M5 generation provides concrete empirical anchor. 50M unit commercial shipping bar appropriately demanding.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF decade-long barrier persists (H4: structural limits block parity before 2034), THEN on-device AI regime bifurcates into two tiers: premium/flagship devices get local frontier inference first, while mid-range and budget segments remain cloud-dependent indefinitely, entrenching digital inequality by 2030.
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: By end-2030, frontier models run locally only on devices above 1500 USD MSRP; cloud-inference market share in developing nations stays above 80%; smartphone market splits into frontier-local capable (top 10% price tier) vs. cloud-dependent (bottom 90%), persisting through 2030.

criticVerdicts.reasoning: Pure drift-forward: hardware economics, manufacturing cost curves, consumer market segmentation all support tiered-device scenario. 66% reasonable.
Digital inequality entrenching through device price tiers is canonical second-order consequence. 0.66 reasonable, possibly understated.

Driven by named corporate actors (Apple, Samsung, Qualcomm) with clear incentive to gate premium features to high-ASP devices. Standard product differentiation rooted in margin protection. Cloud providers benefit from perpetuating cloud dependency. 66% defensible.

Structurally correct from OCHRE's lens. Device price-tier stratification for compute-intensive features is well-documented path-dependent dynamic in consumer electronics. If H4 holds, bifurcation is near-certain. 0.66 appropriate.

Digital inequality framing of on-device AI bifurcation is absent from iid 521's consensus-path treatment. $1,500 MSRP threshold and 80% cloud-dependence metric in developing nations are concrete and differentiating.
